sections.title: Business Overview & Competitive Position
required_elements: Company description (what it does, customer segments, revenue model)
Revenue model classification (subscription, transactional, project-based, licensing, hybrid)
Market position and share (leader, challenger, niche, emerging)
Key competitive advantages (brand, IP, network effects, switching costs, scale, regulatory moat)
Management quality (founder-led vs. professional, tenure, insider ownership)
Customer concentration risk (top 5 customers as % of revenue, >30% = material risk)

sections.title: Financial Analysis
required_elements: Revenue decomposition by segment/product/geography
3-year revenue growth trajectory and drivers
Gross margin analysis and trend (expanding, stable, compressing)
Operating leverage analysis (SG&A and R&D as % of revenue, trajectory)
Free cash flow generation and conversion (FCF/Net Income ratio)
Balance sheet health (net debt, debt/EBITDA, interest coverage, maturity schedule)
Capital allocation (dividends, buybacks, M&A, capex as % of CFO)
Working capital efficiency (DSO, DIO, DPO trends)
optional_elements: Unit economics (CAC, LTV, payback period) if applicable

sections.title: Valuation
required_elements: Current trading multiples (P/E, EV/EBITDA, EV/Revenue, P/FCF)
Historical multiple range (3-year) and current position within range
Peer comparison (at least 3 comparables with same multiples)
Forward estimates (consensus revenue and EPS for next 2 fiscal years)
Valuation methodology appropriate to business model (high-growth: EV/Revenue; mature: P/E, EV/EBITDA; asset-heavy: P/B)
optional_elements: DCF with sensitivity table (growth x discount rate x terminal multiple)

sections.title: Growth Drivers & Catalysts
required_elements: 3-5 near-term catalysts (0-12 months) with expected timing
Secular tailwinds supporting multi-year growth thesis
Product roadmap or expansion strategy
Regulatory or policy catalysts (positive or negative)
optional_elements: M&A pipeline or strategic partnership potential

sections.title: Risk Assessment
required_elements: Top 3-5 material risks ranked by severity and probability
Competitive threats (who could displace, how)
Regulatory/legal risks specific to company
Macro sensitivity (interest rate, FX, commodity, demand cycle exposure)
Key-person risk and succession planning
Thesis invalidation condition (specific condition that breaks the case)
optional_elements: Short interest and short thesis analysis

sections.title: Pattern Matching & Interesting Data Points
required_elements: Flag anomalies, divergences, or non-obvious patterns in the data
Comparisons to historical analogs if applicable
Discrepancies between narrative and financial reality
optional_elements: Options market positioning (put/call skew, unusual activity)

sections.title: Investigation Tracks
required_elements: 2-3 high-value investigation tracks identified
For each track: hypothesis, alpha rationale, data needed
These should be questions that would most change conviction if answered

structural_requirements: Report must begin with Executive Summary (3-5 sentences)
All financial claims must cite sources
Report must end with Opinion block (YAML) and Summary for Perspectives (400-700 words)
Minimum 500 words total
Use markdown headers for all sections (A, B, C, D, E, F, G)
Include at least one data table or comparison
Business model classification explicit (subscription, transactional, project-based, licensing, hybrid)
Market position clearly stated (leader, challenger, niche, emerging)
Competitive advantages enumerated (brand, IP, network effects, switching costs, scale, regulatory)
Customer concentration quantified (top 5 customers as % of revenue)
Revenue decomposition by segment/product/geography
3-year growth trajectory documented with drivers explained
Gross margin calculated and trended (expanding, stable, or compressing)
SG&A and R&D as % of revenue calculated with trend analysis
FCF calculated with FCF/Net Income conversion ratio
Net debt position and leverage ratio (debt/EBITDA) documented
Capital allocation split (% dividends, buybacks, M&A, capex)
Current trading multiples calculated (P/E, EV/EBITDA, EV/Revenue, P/FCF)
Historical multiple range (3-year) shown with current position identified
3 peer companies selected with same multiples calculated
Consensus forward revenue and EPS estimates provided
Valuation methodology justified for business model
Near-term catalysts enumerated (3-5) with timing and expected impact
Secular tailwinds explained
Material risks ranked by severity and probability (top 3-5)
Competitive displacement scenarios described
Macro sensitivity quantified (interest rate, FX, commodity exposure)
Investigation tracks explain specific hypotheses and alpha potential
Data sources clearly cited throughout
